info.title: Partner Directory API
info.description:
The Partner Directory API enables organizations to manage partnerships within the epilot platform.

- **Partner Directory Management**: Maintain a directory of partner organizations that collaborate with your business
- **Partner Invitations**: Invite external organizations to become partners through a secure token-based invitation flow
- **Assignable Search**: Find users and organizations that can be assigned to tasks, workflows, or entities across your organization and partners
- **Partner User Management**: Manage users within partner organizations, including creating users and assigning roles
- **Geolocation Services**: Convert addresses to geographic coordinates for partner location-based features

tags.name: Partners
tags.description:
Operations for managing the partner directory including:
- Inviting new partners to collaborate
- Approving or rejecting partner requests
- Searching for assignable users and organizations across partners
- Looking up partners by invitation token
- Converting addresses to geolocation coordinates for partner location features

tags.name: Partner Users
tags.description:
Operations for managing users within partner organizations:
- Listing users in a partner organization
- Creating new users in partner organizations
- Deleting users from partner organizations
- Managing roles and role assignments for partner users

These operations allow Vendor organizations to control access and permissions for users in their partner network.

post.description:
Approves a pending partner request, allowing the partner to begin collaboration.

When a partner requests to join your organization's partner network, this endpoint
is used to approve the request and activate the partnership.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the partner to approve

400.description: Unable to approve partner - partner may already be approved or in an invalid state

post.description:
Rejects a pending partner request, declining the partnership.

When a partner requests to join your organization's partner network, this endpoint
is used to reject the request if the partnership is not desired.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the partner to reject

400.description: Unable to reject partner - partner may already be rejected or in an invalid state

post.description:
Search for users and organizations that can be assigned to tasks, workflows, or entities.

This endpoint searches across your organization and all connected partner organizations
to find assignable resources. Use this when you need to assign someone to a task,
workflow step, or entity and want to include partners in the search.

**Results can include:**
- `user`: Users in your organization
- `partner_user`: Users in partner organizations
- `partner_organization`: Partner organizations themselves (for organization-level assignments)
- `ecp`: End Customer Portal users
- `group`: User groups
- `parent_organization_user`: Users from parent organization (for sub-organizations)

**Pagination:** Use `from` and `size` parameters for paginated results.

q.description: Search query string to filter results by name or email

size.description: Maximum number of results to return per page

org_ids.description: Filter results to specific organization IDs. If not provided, searches all accessible organizations including partners.

portalUsersEntityIdScope.description:
Entity ID to scope portal user search. Required when `types` includes `ecp`.
When provided, only portal users related to the specified entity are returned
instead of placeholder users.

types.description:
Filter results to specific types of assignables.
If not provided, defaults to all types except `parent_organization_user`.

200.description: List of assignable results matching the search criteria

hits.description: Total number of matching results (may exceed returned results for pagination)

post.description:
Retrieve multiple assignable users or groups by their IDs in a single request.

Use this endpoint when you have a list of user or group IDs and need to fetch
their full assignable information. This is more efficient than making multiple
individual requests.

Each item in the request array should specify either a `user_id` or `group_id`.

user_id.description: User ID of the assignable to retrieve

org_id.description: Organization ID of the assignable. If not provided, defaults to the caller's organization.

group_id.description: Group ID of the assignable to retrieve (mutually exclusive with user_id)

200.description: List of assignable results for the requested IDs

400.description: Invalid request - empty array or malformed request body

get.description:
Retrieves partner information using an invitation token.

This is a **public endpoint** that does not require authentication.
It is used during the partner onboarding flow to display partner information
before the invited organization completes their registration.

The token is sent to the partner via email when they are invited.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The invitation token received via email

404.description: Token not found or expired - the invitation may have already been used or is no longer valid

post.description:
Activates a partner account using an invitation token.

This is a **public endpoint** that does not require authentication.
It is called when an invited partner completes their registration to establish
the partnership between the two organizations.

**Activation Flow:**
1. Partner receives invitation email with token
2. Partner clicks link and views invitation details (via `getPartnerByToken`)
3. Partner completes registration form
4. This endpoint is called to activate the partnership

After activation, the partner organization can begin collaborating with
the inviting organization through shared entities and assignments.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The invitation token received via email

200.description: Partner activated successfully - the partnership is now active

400.description: Invalid request - missing required fields or invalid data

404.description: Token not found or expired - the invitation may have already been used or is no longer valid

409.description: Partner already activated - this token has already been used

post.description:
Converts an address string to geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude).

This endpoint is used to geocode partner addresses for location-based features such as:
- Partner search by proximity
- Displaying partners on a map
- Calculating activity radius coverage

The address should be provided as a complete, well-formatted string for best results.

400.description: Invalid request - address field is missing or empty

404.description: Address could not be geocoded - no matching location found for the provided address

post.description:
Sends an invitation email to a partner organization to begin collaboration.

This endpoint generates an invitation token and sends an email to the partner
with a link to complete their registration. The partner can then accept the
invitation to establish a B2B partnership.

**Invitation Flow:**
1. Create a partner record (via entity API)
2. Call this endpoint to send the invitation email
3. Partner receives email with unique invitation link
4. Partner activates their account via the public activation endpoint

The invitation email language can be customized via the request body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the partner to invite

400.description: Unable to send invitation - partner may already be invited or email configuration is invalid

get.description:
Retrieves all users belonging to a partner organization along with their assigned roles.

This endpoint allows parent organizations to view and manage the users within
their partner organizations. The response includes user details and their
role assignments within the partner organization.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner whose users to retrieve

200.description: List of partner users with their roles

400.description: Invalid request - organization ID may be invalid or not a partner organization

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to view users in this partner organization

post.description:
Creates a new user in a partner organization.

This endpoint allows parent organizations to create users directly within
their partner organizations. The new user will receive an invitation email
to set up their account.

Optionally, roles can be assigned to the user at creation time.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The partner organization ID where the user will be created

201.description: User created successfully - an invitation email has been sent

400.description: Invalid request - email may be invalid or user already exists in the organization

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to create users in this partner organization

delete.description:
Removes a user from a partner organization.

This endpoint allows parent organizations to delete users from their partner
organizations. The user will lose access to the partner organization immediately.

**Note:** This action is permanent and cannot be undone.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to delete

400.description: Invalid request - user ID may be invalid

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to delete users in this partner organization

404.description: User or partner organization not found

get.description:
Retrieves all roles defined for a partner organization.

Roles define the permissions that users in the partner organization have.
Parent organizations can use this endpoint to view available roles before
assigning them to partner users.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner whose roles to retrieve

200.description: List of roles available in the partner organization

400.description: Invalid request - organization ID may be invalid

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to view roles in this partner organization

post.description:
Creates a new role for a partner organization.

Roles define permissions for partner users. The parent organization can create
custom roles with specific permission grants to control what partner users
can access and modify.

**Permission Grants:**
Each role contains a list of grants that define allowed (or denied) actions
on specific resources. See the Grant schema for details on defining permissions.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner where the role will be created

400.description: Invalid request - role name or slug may be invalid or already exists

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to create roles in this partner organization

put.description:
Updates an existing role in a partner organization.

Use this endpoint to modify the permissions (grants) or metadata of an existing
role. All users with this role will immediately have the updated permissions.

**Note:** Changing role permissions affects all users currently assigned to the role.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the role to update

400.description: Invalid request - role data may be invalid

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to update roles in this partner organization

404.description: Role or partner organization not found

delete.description: Delete a role from a partner organization

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner

403.description: Forbidden - insufficient permissions or role cannot be deleted

409.description: Role is currently assigned to users and cannot be deleted

post.description:
Assigns one or more roles to a user in a partner organization.

Roles define the permissions that the user has within the partner organization.
Multiple roles can be assigned in a single request. The response indicates
the success or failure of each role assignment.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to assign roles to

200.description: Role assignment results - each role assignment is processed individually

results.description: Results for each role assignment attempt

roleId.description: The role ID that was processed

400.description: Invalid request - role IDs may be invalid

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to assign roles in this partner organization

404.description: User or partner organization not found

delete.description:
Removes one or more roles from a user in a partner organization.

This endpoint removes the specified roles from the user, reducing their permissions
within the partner organization. The response indicates the success or failure
of each role removal.

**Note:** Removing all roles from a user may result in the user having no access
to the partner organization.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The organization ID of the partner

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to remove roles from

200.description: Role unassignment results - each role removal is processed individually

results.description: Results for each role removal attempt

roleId.description: The role ID that was processed

400.description: Invalid request - role IDs may be invalid

403.description: Access denied - caller does not have permission to unassign roles in this partner organization

404.description: User, role, or partner organization not found

EpilotAuth.description: Authorization header with epilot OAuth2 bearer token

AsOrganization.name: x-ivy-org-id
AsOrganization.description: Set organization id when using internal auth

InviteToken.description: A secure token used for partner invitation and activation. Sent via email to the invited partner.

Partner.description:
Represents a partner organization in the partner directory.

Partners go through a lifecycle from invitation to active collaboration:
- **Pending**: Initial state when partner record is created
- **Invited**: Invitation email has been sent to the partner
- **Request**: Partner has requested to join (self-registration)
- **Rejected**: Partnership request was declined
- **Deleted**: Partner relationship has been terminated

organization_id.description: The organization ID of the parent organization that invited this partner

created_at.description: Timestamp when the partner record was created

description.description: Optional description of the partner or partnership
description.example: Regional solar installation partner for Bavaria

company_name.description: The legal or trading name of the partner organization

invitation_token.description: Secure token for partner activation (only present for invited partners)

invitation_email.description: Deprecated: Use 'email' instead. Email address used for the invitation.

email.description: Primary email address of the partner, used for invitation delivery

owner_email.description: Alternative email address where invitations should be sent (e.g., owner or admin email)

signed_up_email.description: Email address used by the partner when completing registration

partner_org_id.description: Organization ID of the partner organization (populated after activation)

status.description: Current status of the partner in the invitation/collaboration lifecycle

PartnerId.description: Unique identifier for a partner record (UUID format)

OrganizationId.description: Unique identifier for an organization in the epilot platform

ActivatePartnerPayload.description: Payload for activating a partner account using an invitation token

company_name.description: The official name of the partner's company

signed_up_email.description: Email address of the user completing the partner registration

organization_id.description: The organization ID of the partner's existing epilot organization (if they already have one)

PartnerInvitationPayload.description: Configuration options for sending a partner invitation

language.description: Language for the partner invitation email. Determines the email template language.

Assignable.description:
A user, organization, or group that can be assigned to tasks, workflows, or entities.
The `type` field discriminates between different assignable types.

BaseAssignable.description: Common properties shared by all assignable types

type.description: Discriminator field indicating the type of assignable

display_name.description: Human-readable name for display in UI (user name, organization name, or group name)

image_uri.description: Profile image URLs for the assignable

thumbnail_32.description: 32x32 pixel thumbnail image URL for compact displays

org_id.description: Organization ID the assignable belongs to

created_at.description: Timestamp when the assignable was created

activated_at.description: Timestamp when the assignable was activated (for users, when they accepted their invitation)

AssignableUser.description: A user within the caller's organization that can be assigned to tasks or entities

AssignablePartnerUser.description: A user from a partner organization that can be assigned to shared tasks or entities

display_name.description: Full name of the partner user

partner_id.description: ID of the partner relationship through which this user is accessible

user_id.description: Unique identifier of the partner user

email.description: Email address of the partner user

AssignableGroup.description: A user group that can be assigned to tasks or entities. All members of the group will be notified/assigned.

group_id.description: Unique identifier of the user group

AssignableOrganization.description:
A partner organization that can be assigned to tasks or entities at the organization level.
Useful when you want to assign work to a partner company rather than a specific individual.

email.description: Primary contact email for the partner organization

geolocations.description: Physical locations of the partner organization with addresses and coordinates

phone.description: Primary contact phone number for the partner organization

activity_radius.description: Geographic service radius in kilometers - the area within which the partner operates

AssignableEcpPlaceholder.description:
An End Customer Portal (ECP) user that can be assigned to tasks or entities.
These are external users who access the system through the customer portal.

user_id.description: Unique identifier of the portal user

email.description: Email address of the portal user

SearchGeolocation.description: Request payload for geocoding an address to coordinates

address.description:
Full address string to convert to geographic coordinates.
For best results, include street, city, postal code, and country.

addressLabel.description: Normalized full address as returned by the geocoding service

relevance.description:
Confidence score for the geocoding result (0-1).
Values closer to 1 indicate higher confidence that the coordinates match the input address.

AddressGeolocation.description: Combined address and geographic coordinates for a location

PartnerUser.description: A user within a partner organization, including their assigned roles

status.description:
Current status of the user account:
- Active: User has completed registration and can access the system
- Pending: User has been invited but not yet completed registration
- Deactivated: User account has been disabled

image.description: Profile image URLs for the user

roles.description: List of roles assigned to the user within the partner organization

CreatePartnerUserPayload.description: Request payload for creating a new user in a partner organization

email.description: Email address for the new user. An invitation will be sent to this address.

language.description: Preferred language for the user. Determines the language of the invitation email and default UI language.

roles.description:
Role IDs to automatically assign to the user upon creation.
If not provided, the user will have no roles until manually assigned.

CreatePartnerRolePayload.description: Request payload for creating a new role in a partner organization

name.description: Human-readable display name for the role

slug.description: URL-friendly identifier for the role (lowercase, underscores allowed)

grants.description:
Permission grants that define what users with this role can do.
Each grant specifies an action and resource pattern.

UpdatePartnerRolePayload.description: Request payload for updating an existing role in a partner organization

grants.description: Updated list of permission grants for the role

Grant.description:
A permission grant that allows or denies a specific action on a resource.

Grants are the building blocks of roles and define what users can do within the system.

action.description:
The action being granted or denied.
Common actions include: entity-read, entity-create, entity-update, entity-delete

resource.description:
The resource pattern this grant applies to.
Format: entity:<org_id>:<schema>:<entity_id>
Use wildcards (*) to match multiple resources.

effect.description: Whether this grant allows or denies the action

conditions.description: Additional conditions that must be met for this grant to apply

GrantWithDependencies.description: A grant with optional dependent grants that are automatically included when this grant is assigned

dependencies.description:
Additional grants that are automatically included when this grant is assigned.
Dependencies are expanded and stored with the role to ensure users have all
necessary permissions for the primary action.

GrantCondition.description:
An additional condition that must be met for a grant to apply.
Conditions allow fine-grained control over when permissions are active.

EqualsCondition.description:
A condition that checks if an attribute equals one of the specified values.
The grant only applies when the condition is satisfied.

attribute.description:
The attribute path to check (dot notation for nested attributes).
Example: workflows.primary.task_name checks the task name in the primary workflow.

values.description: List of values to match against - the condition is true if the attribute equals any of these values

RoleId.description:
Unique identifier for a role, combining organization ID and role slug.
Format: <organization_id>:<slug>

BaseRoleForCreate.description: Base schema for creating or updating a role

name.description: Human-readable display name for the role

slug.description: URL-friendly identifier for the role (lowercase, no spaces)

grants.description: List of permission grants that define what users with this role can do

PartnerRole.description: A role definition for users in a partner organization

id.description: Unique identifier for the role (format: org_id:slug)

name.description: Human-readable display name for the role

type.description:
Type of role. Partner roles are typically 'share_role' indicating
they are designed for cross-organization sharing scenarios.

grants.description: List of permission grants that define what users with this role can do

AssignRolesPayload.description: Request payload for assigning or unassigning roles to/from a user

roleIds.description: Array of role IDs to assign or unassign

User.description: A user account in the epilot platform

display_name.description: Full name of the user for display purposes

status.description:
Current status of the user account:
- Active: User has completed registration and can access the system
- Pending: User has been invited but not yet completed registration
- Deactivated: User account has been disabled
